title: Save Our Small Farms Act of 2025
short_title: Save Our Small Farms Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill makes changes to a government program that helps farmers when their crops get damaged and they don't have insurance. The changes will make it easier for small farms and farms that grow many different types of crops to apply for help and get money when they need it. The bill also creates a way for these farmers to get better insurance in the future with discounts and extra support to help them pay for it.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Save Our Small Farms Act of twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2  Administration and Operation of Noninsured Crop Assistance Program
sections.text: This section changes how the government runs a program called the noninsured crop assistance program. This program helps farmers when their crops are damaged but they do not have crop insurance. The changes make the program work better by adding new goals. The program must now help more types of crops get added to the official list with local prices. It must help farmers voluntarily move to a bigger insurance plan called the whole farm revenue insurance plan. It must also create test programs to try new ideas and collect information. The government will create an easier application process for farmers who grow many different crops, small farms, and farmers who sell directly to customers. These farmers can file fewer reports and can submit reports twice a year instead of more often. The government will create a special option to help farmers transition from the assistance program to the whole farm insurance plan if they want to. Farmers who promise to switch within three years get a twenty five percent discount on their premium the first year. Farmers who promise to switch within two years get a fifty percent discount the second year. Farmers who actually buy the whole farm plan get a fifty percent discount the third year. Farmers can use their tax form Schedule F to show their farm income history. The government must share this income information with the Federal Crop Insurance Corporation. The Secretary of Agriculture has ninety days after this law passes to write rules about these discounts. Farmers who grow crops that are picked by hand or spoil quickly can report crop losses anytime after one hundred twenty hours pass since the loss happened. When farmers need someone to look at their damaged crops, they have options if a loss adjuster cannot come within seventy two hours. They can use remote methods like photos with time stamps or drone videos. They can also have trained local office staff look at the damage along with remote methods. The government must train local office staff to do these damage assessments. The program will now pay one hundred percent of costs instead of sixty five percent. The program increases the maximum coverage amount to six hundred thousand dollars for certain types of farmers. This includes farmers with limited resources, beginning farmers, socially disadvantaged farmers, veteran farmers, and farmers using the special transition option. These same groups of farmers also get premium discounts. They only pay twenty five percent of the normal premium cost. The government will work with outreach groups, extension offices, and state agriculture departments to advertise this program to the farmers who can benefit most from it.
